article_meta.title: Tillis: 'Noem Is Out of Her Depth,' She's 'Not Competent' 7 Pam Key28 Jan 2026 Wednesday on CNN's "The Lead," Sen

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Tillis: 'Noem Is Out of Her Depth,' She's 'Not Competent' 7 Pam Key28 Jan 2026 Wednesday on CNN's "The Lead," Sen
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

Tillis: 'Noem Is Out of Her Depth,' She's 'Not Competent' 7 Pam Key28 Jan 2026 Wednesday on CNN's "The Lead," Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC) said Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem "is not competent" to run the federal agency. Tillis said, "Well, I think they're doing the American people and the president a great disservice by not realizing how much attention is focused on these operations and how details and discipline matter. They spoke before they knew anything about what occurred on the ground. They didn't even implement the typical protocol that you would. And an officer involved shooting as much to protect those officers as the scene of the incident." He added, "Everything that goes into an investigation after a casualty, after a death. They talked too soon. It looked like they wanted to get ahead of a news cycle. And it really discredits it. It brings their credibility into question. And, Jake, it's very important for people in North Carolina to know I've held every sheriff in North Carolina that was a sanctuary city, jurisdiction accountable. We went after them. Our office filed a bill for justice for sanctuary, a justice for victims of sanctuary cities, to make sure that if somebody was harmed by someone who was detained and not transferred to ICE, that they could sue that local government and get restitution. So I've made it very clear that I want law enforcement to work with each other, but you can't be situational. You can't expect that out of a Biden administration and Democrat mayors, and then not expect us to lead by example by coordinating with state and local law enforcement. I believe that Noem is out of her depth. I think she's reached the Peter Principle. She is not competent to run this organization, and I haven't even begun to talk about the failure of FEMA and disaster response in North Carolina, but I will be tomorrow on the floor." Follow Pam Key on X @pamkeyNEN ClipsPoliticsDonald TrumpKristi NoemStephen MillerThom Tillis
---

```json
{
  "summary": "Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC) sharply criticized Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem during an appearance on CNN's \"The Lead,\" declaring her incompetent and out of her depth in running the federal agency. Tillis condemned the handling of an officer-involved shooting incident, stating that officials spoke prematurely without knowing ground details and failed to follow standard protocols. He accused them of trying to get ahead of the news cycle, which damaged their credibility. While emphasizing his own record of holding sanctuary jurisdictions accountable in North Carolina, Tillis argued that federal leadership must coordinate properly with state and local law enforcement and lead by example. He invoked the \"Peter Principle,\" suggesting Noem has been promoted beyond her competence level. Tillis also mentioned plans to address FEMA's failure in North Carolina disaster response on the Senate floor, indicating broader concerns about the agency's performance under Noem's leadership.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Senator Thom Tillis called Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem \"not competent\" and \"out of her depth\" on CNN",
    "Tillis criticized the agency's handling of an officer-involved shooting, stating officials spoke before knowing ground details and violated standard protocols",
    "The senator accused leadership of prioritizing getting ahead of the news cycle over proper investigation procedures",
    "Tillis emphasized his record of holding sanctuary cities accountable in North Carolina and stressed the need for federal-state-local law enforcement coordination",
    "Tillis announced plans to address FEMA's failure in North Carolina disaster response on the Senate floor"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC)",
    "Breitbart News reporting"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC) sharply criticized Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem during an appearance on CNN's "The Lead," declaring her incompetent and out of her depth in running the federal agency. Tillis condemned the handling of an officer-involved shooting incident, stating that officials spoke prematurely without knowing ground details and failed to follow standard protocols. He accused them of trying to get ahead of the news cycle, which damaged their credibility. While emphasizing his own record of holding sanctuary jurisdictions accountable in North Carolina, Tillis argued that federal leadership must coordinate properly with state and local law enforcement and lead by example. He invoked the "Peter Principle," suggesting Noem has been promoted beyond her competence level. Tillis also mentioned plans to address FEMA's failure in North Carolina disaster response on the Senate floor, indicating broader concerns about the agency's performance under Noem's leadership.
key_facts: Senator Thom Tillis called Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem "not competent" and "out of her depth" on CNN
Tillis criticized the agency's handling of an officer-involved shooting, stating officials spoke before knowing ground details and violated standard protocols
The senator accused leadership of prioritizing getting ahead of the news cycle over proper investigation procedures
Tillis emphasized his record of holding sanctuary cities accountable in North Carolina and stressed the need for federal-state-local law enforcement coordination
Tillis announced plans to address FEMA's failure in North Carolina disaster response on the Senate floor
